title: Tillandsia ionochroma
taxonomy: Tillandsia ionochroma is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. This epiphyte is native to Mexico and is part of the Tillandsia genus, which includes around 650 species of air plants. Tillandsia ionochroma is a relatively rare species and is known for its vibrant, long-lasting flowers. The plant's classification places it within the order Poales and the class Liliopsida. Its unique characteristics and growth habits have made it a popular choice among collectors and enthusiasts.

intro: Tillandsia ionochroma, also known as the Red Vase Plant, is a species of epiphytic bromeliad native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in wound healing, reducing inflammation, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Tillandsia ionochroma include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Tillandsia ionochroma include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and skin conditions.
